title: Apple Computer Co. founded
description: Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne signed the Apple Computer Company partnership agreement.

title: Ronald Wayne sells his stake
description: Ronald Wayne sold his 10% stake in Apple for $800, a decision that would later be worth billions.

title: Apple I goes on sale
description: The Apple I, designed by Steve Wozniak, went on sale for $666.66 through the Byte Shop.

title: Apple Computer Inc. incorporated
description: Apple Computer was formally incorporated with Mike Markkula joining as investor and co-founder.

title: Apple II unveiled at West Coast Computer Faire
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the Apple II at the first West Coast Computer Faire in San Francisco.

title: Apple II goes on sale
description: The Apple II officially went on sale for $1,298, becoming one of the first mass-market personal computers.

title: Apple Disk II released
description: Apple released the Disk II floppy drive for $495, dramatically improving the usability of the Apple II.

title: VisiCalc ships for Apple II
description: VisiCalc, the first killer app, shipped exclusively for the Apple II, driving enormous sales of the computer.

title: Jobs visits Xerox PARC
description: Steve Jobs and Apple engineers visited Xerox PARC and were shown the graphical user interface and mouse, influencing all future Apple products.

title: Apple goes public
description: Apple's IPO raised $101 million, the largest IPO since Ford Motor Company in 1956, creating more millionaires than any company in history at the time.

title: Apple III re-released
description: Apple re-released a revised Apple III after recalling the original due to serious hardware flaws.

title: Apple Lisa launched
description: Apple launched the Lisa, the first commercial personal computer with a graphical user interface, priced at $9,995.

title: John Sculley joins Apple as CEO
description: John Sculley left PepsiCo to become Apple CEO after Jobs famously asked him: 'Do you want to sell sugar water for the rest of your life?'

title: 1984 ad airs during Super Bowl
description: Apple's iconic '1984' ad directed by Ridley Scott aired during Super Bowl XVIII, widely considered the greatest commercial ever made.

title: Macintosh introduced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the original Macintosh at Apple's annual shareholder meeting, pulling it from a bag and letting it introduce itself.

title: Apple introduces LaserWriter
description: Apple launched the LaserWriter printer alongside PageMaker, effectively creating the desktop publishing industry.

title: Steve Jobs forced out of Mac division
description: Following a boardroom power struggle with John Sculley, Steve Jobs was stripped of his role overseeing the Macintosh division.

title: Steve Jobs resigns from Apple
description: Steve Jobs resigned from Apple and announced he was founding a new computer company called NeXT.

title: Pixar spun off from Lucasfilm
description: Steve Jobs purchased the computer graphics division of Lucasfilm for $10 million, creating Pixar Animation Studios.

title: Macintosh II released
description: Apple released the Macintosh II, the first Mac with a color display and an open architecture with expansion slots.

title: Macintosh IIx released
description: Apple released the Macintosh IIx with a faster processor and FDHD floppy drive.

title: Macintosh Portable released
description: Apple released the Macintosh Portable, its first battery-powered laptop, for $6,500.

title: Macintosh Classic and LC released
description: Apple released the affordable Macintosh Classic at $999 and the Macintosh LC, dramatically expanding the Mac's market reach.

title: System 7 released
description: Apple released System 7, a major Mac OS upgrade adding virtual memory, networking, and TrueType fonts.

title: PowerBook 100 series launched
description: Apple launched the PowerBook 100, 140, and 170, defining the modern laptop form factor with a palm rest and centered trackball.

title: Apple reaches $7 billion in annual sales
description: Apple reported annual revenues exceeding $7 billion, making it the most profitable personal computer maker in the world.

title: Apple Newton MessagePad released
description: Apple released the Newton MessagePad, one of the first PDAs, pioneering handwriting recognition and mobile computing.

title: John Sculley resigns as CEO
description: John Sculley resigned as Apple CEO after a decade leading the company, replaced by Michael Spindler.

title: Power Macintosh line launched
description: Apple launched the Power Macintosh line, transitioning from Motorola 68k to PowerPC processors.

title: Apple licenses Mac OS to clone makers
description: Apple announced it would license the Mac OS to third-party manufacturers, creating the short-lived Mac clone market.

title: Copland OS project begun
description: Apple began serious development on Copland, an ambitious next-generation Mac OS that would ultimately be cancelled.

title: Michael Spindler replaced by Gil Amelio
description: Apple's board replaced CEO Michael Spindler with Gil Amelio, a semiconductor industry veteran, as the company continued to struggle.

title: Apple acquires NeXT
description: Apple announced the $429 million acquisition of NeXT, bringing Steve Jobs back to Apple and the foundation for Mac OS X.

title: Apple posts $120M quarterly loss
description: Apple reported a $120 million loss for Q1 1997, signaling the depth of its financial crisis before Steve Jobs returned.

title: Gil Amelio ousted as CEO
description: Apple's board forced Gil Amelio to resign after 500 days as CEO, with Steve Jobs stepping in as interim CEO.

title: Steve Jobs ends clone licensing program
description: Steve Jobs terminated Apple's Mac clone licensing agreements, ending the brief era of third-party Mac manufacturers.

title: Microsoft invests $150M in Apple
description: At Macworld Boston, Steve Jobs announced Microsoft would invest $150 million in Apple and commit to Office for Mac, shocking the tech world.

title: Steve Jobs named interim CEO
description: Steve Jobs officially became Apple's interim CEO, starting the turnaround that would make Apple the most valuable company in the world.

title: Apple Store online launches
description: Apple launched its online store at apple.com, a key part of Jobs' strategy to control the sales experience.

title: Steve Jobs kills Newton
description: Steve Jobs cancelled the Newton MessagePad product line, one of his first major decisions as interim CEO.

title: iMac G3 announced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the colorful iMac G3 at a press event, declaring 'iMac. Hello again.' and beginning Apple's design renaissance.

title: iMac G3 goes on sale
description: The translucent Bondi Blue iMac G3 went on sale for $1,299, selling 800,000 units in its first five months.

title: iBook launched
description: Apple launched the iBook consumer laptop in a clamshell design with built-in AirPort Wi-Fi for $1,599.

title: iMac DV introduced with iMovie
description: Apple released the iMac DV with a DVD drive and bundled iMovie, launching the digital hub strategy.

title: Steve Jobs becomes permanent CEO
description: Steve Jobs dropped 'interim' from his title and became Apple's permanent CEO at Macworld Expo San Francisco.

title: Mac OS X Public Beta previewed
description: Steve Jobs previewed Mac OS X at Macworld Expo, showing the Aqua interface for the first time.

title: iTools launches with free .mac email
description: Apple launched iTools, offering free .mac email addresses, iDisk cloud storage, and HomePage website builder.

title: iTunes launched
description: Steve Jobs unveiled iTunes at Macworld Expo, calling it 'the best jukebox software in the world.'

title: Mac OS X beta ships to developers
description: Apple shipped the Mac OS X Public Beta to developers, charging $29.95 for early access to the new operating system.

title: PowerBook G4 Titanium unveiled
description: Apple unveiled the PowerBook G4 Titanium at Macworld, the thinnest and most powerful laptop of its time.

title: Mac OS X released
description: Apple released Mac OS X 10.0 Cheetah, a complete reimagining of the Mac operating system built on a Unix foundation.

title: First Apple Retail Stores open
description: Apple opened its first two retail stores in McLean, Virginia and Glendale, California, against widespread industry skepticism.

title: Apple halts all operations after 9/11 attacks
description: Apple closed all US retail stores and halted operations following the September 11 terrorist attacks in New York and Washington.

title: iPod launched
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the original iPod, saying '1,000 songs in your pocket.' The $399 device would transform the music industry.

title: iPod goes on sale
description: The original iPod went on sale for $399, compatible only with Macs.

title: iMac G4 'lamp' released
description: Apple released the iMac G4 with an iconic floating flat-panel display on an arm, nicknamed 'the lamp.'

title: Xcode released
description: Apple released Xcode, replacing Project Builder as the primary IDE for Mac and later iOS development.

title: iTunes Music Store opens
description: Apple opened the iTunes Music Store with 200,000 songs at 99 cents each, selling one million songs in the first week.

title: iTunes for Windows released
description: Apple released iTunes for Windows, opening the iPod and iTunes ecosystem to the majority of PC users.

title: iTunes Music Store surpasses 25 million songs sold
description: Apple announced iTunes had sold 25 million songs in its first eight months, averaging 3 million per month.

title: GarageBand introduced
description: Steve Jobs introduced GarageBand at Macworld Expo, democratizing music production for everyday users.

title: iTunes for Windows reaches 50 million songs sold
description: Apple celebrated iTunes for Windows surpassing 50 million songs sold just months after its October 2003 launch.

title: EU opens antitrust investigation into Apple
description: The European Commission opened a formal antitrust investigation into Apple's App Store practices and Apple Pay.

title: Steve Jobs reveals he has cancer
description: Steve Jobs sent an email to Apple employees revealing he had been diagnosed with a treatable form of pancreatic cancer.

title: iPod Photo released
description: Apple released the iPod Photo, the first iPod capable of displaying color photos on its screen.

title: Mac mini introduced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the Mac mini at Macworld, a $499 headless Mac designed to attract Windows switchers.

title: Mac mini goes on sale
description: The Mac mini went on sale for $499, Apple's most affordable desktop computer designed to attract Windows switchers.

title: Apple announces switch to Intel
description: Steve Jobs announced at WWDC that Apple would transition the Mac lineup from PowerPC to Intel processors.

title: iPod nano announced
description: Steve Jobs pulled the iPod nano out of the small pocket in his jeans, unveiling the impossibly thin flash-based music player.

title: First Intel Macs released
description: Apple released the first Intel-based MacBook Pro and iMac at Macworld, completing the Intel transition ahead of schedule.

title: iMac transitions to Intel Core Duo
description: Apple updated the iMac to Intel Core Duo processors, the first consumer Mac to complete the Intel transition.

title: Boot Camp released for Intel Macs
description: Apple released Boot Camp, allowing Intel Mac users to install and run Windows natively for the first time.

title: Mac Pro introduced
description: Apple introduced the Mac Pro tower, the last Mac to transition to Intel, replacing the Power Mac G5.

title: Apple applies for iPhone trademark
description: Apple filed trademark applications for the iPhone name ahead of its announcement, facing an initial dispute with Cisco.

title: iPhone announced
description: Steve Jobs announced the original iPhone at Macworld, declaring Apple was introducing 'a widescreen iPod, a phone, and an internet communicator.'

title: Apple Computer Inc. renamed Apple Inc.
description: Steve Jobs announced Apple Computer was dropping 'Computer' from its name to become Apple Inc., reflecting its expansion beyond computers.

title: Steve Jobs publishes Thoughts on Music
description: Steve Jobs published an open letter calling on music labels to drop DRM, arguing that open music would benefit everyone.

title: iPhone gets AT&T exclusivity confirmed
description: Apple confirmed a five-year exclusivity deal with AT&T for iPhone in the United States, just weeks before launch.

title: iPhone goes on sale
description: The original iPhone went on sale at Apple Stores across the US, with crowds lining up for days. It sold 6 million units in its first year.

title: iPhone activations surge past 1 million
description: Just days after launch, iPhone activations surpassed one million, far exceeding Apple's expectations for the revolutionary device.

title: Mac OS X Leopard released
description: Apple released Mac OS X 10.5 Leopard with over 300 new features including Time Machine and Boot Camp.

title: iTunes movie rentals announced
description: Apple announced iTunes movie rentals at Macworld, partnering with all major studios to offer HD film rentals.

title: MacBook Air introduced
description: Steve Jobs pulled the MacBook Air out of a manila envelope at Macworld, unveiling the world's thinnest laptop.

title: iTunes passes Walmart as top US music retailer
description: Apple announced iTunes had surpassed Walmart to become the number one music retailer in the United States.

title: Apple removes DRM from iTunes music
description: Apple began selling DRM-free music from EMI on iTunes, the first step toward fully unlocking the iTunes library.

title: iPhone SDK launched
description: Apple released the iPhone SDK, allowing third-party developers to build apps for the iPhone for the first time.

title: MobileMe launched to replace .Mac
description: Apple announced MobileMe, a paid cloud sync service replacing .Mac, though its troubled launch drew rare public criticism from Steve Jobs.

title: iPhone 3G pricing revealed
description: Apple revealed the iPhone 3G would start at $199 with a two-year AT&T contract, making it significantly more affordable.

title: App Store opens
description: Apple launched the App Store with 500 apps alongside iPhone OS 2.0, creating a new multi-billion dollar industry.

title: iPhone 3G released
description: Apple released the iPhone 3G with 3G data support and GPS for $199, making it the first truly affordable iPhone.

title: App Store hits 10 million downloads in first weekend
description: Apple announced the App Store was downloaded 10 million times in its first three days, far exceeding expectations.

title: Steve Jobs takes medical leave
description: Steve Jobs announced a medical leave of absence, with Tim Cook taking over day-to-day operations.

title: iTunes Store launches in more countries
description: Apple expanded the iTunes Store to 12 additional countries, bringing its total to over 50 markets worldwide.

title: iPhone OS 3.0 announced with copy and paste
description: Apple announced iPhone OS 3.0 at a press event, finally adding copy and paste, MMS, and push notifications.

title: iPhone 3.0 software preview held
description: Apple held a special press event previewing iPhone OS 3.0 features including cut, copy, paste and push notifications.

title: iPhone OS 3.0 beta 3 released
description: Apple seeded iPhone OS 3.0 beta 3 to developers, refining features ahead of the summer public release.

title: iTunes Store sells 1 billion apps
description: Apple announced the App Store surpassed one billion downloads just nine months after its July 2008 launch.

title: iPhone 3GS announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 3GS at WWDC with video recording, voice control, and faster performance.

title: Steve Jobs returns from medical leave
description: Steve Jobs returned to Apple after a six-month medical leave during which he received a liver transplant.

title: iTunes goes DRM-free
description: Apple completed its transition to DRM-free music in iTunes, selling all songs without copy protection.

title: iPhone reaches 3 billion App Store downloads
description: Apple announced the App Store surpassed 3 billion downloads, just 18 months after launch.

title: Apple surpasses Microsoft in market cap
description: Apple's market capitalization surpassed Microsoft's for the first time, marking a historic reversal of fortunes.

title: iPad announced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the original iPad, saying it was 'a magical and revolutionary device' that sat between a smartphone and a laptop.

title: iPad pre-orders open
description: Apple opened pre-orders for the original iPad, with the Wi-Fi model starting at $499.

title: iPad sells 300,000 units on launch day
description: Apple announced the original iPad sold 300,000 units and downloaded one million apps on its very first day.

title: iPad goes on sale
description: The original iPad went on sale starting at $499, selling 300,000 units on its first day and 1 million within 28 days.

title: iPhone 4 announced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the iPhone 4 at WWDC, featuring a radical new glass and stainless steel design and the Retina display.

title: iPhone 4 pre-orders crash Apple website
description: iPhone 4 pre-orders caused Apple's website and AT&T's systems to crash under unprecedented demand.

title: iPhone 4 released
description: The iPhone 4 went on sale, with lines stretching around Apple Stores globally. It would later become famous for 'Antennagate.'

title: Apple holds Antennagate press conference
description: Steve Jobs held a press conference addressing iPhone 4 antenna issues, offering free cases and refunds to dissatisfied customers.

title: MacBook Air redesigned
description: Apple released a completely redesigned MacBook Air in 11 and 13-inch sizes, defining the ultrabook category.

title: Mac App Store launches
description: Apple launched the Mac App Store, bringing iPhone-style app distribution to the desktop.

title: Verizon iPhone goes on sale
description: The iPhone 4 launched on Verizon for the first time, ending AT&T's exclusive deal and massively expanding iPhone's US market.

title: App Store hits 10 billion downloads
description: Apple celebrated the App Store reaching 10 billion downloads, just two and a half years after launch.

title: iPad 2 announced
description: Steve Jobs made a surprise appearance to announce the iPad 2, thinner and faster with front and rear cameras.

title: iCloud announced to developers
description: Apple began briefing developers on its upcoming iCloud service ahead of the WWDC announcement.

title: iCloud previewed to developers at WWDC
description: Apple gave developers their first detailed look at iCloud features ahead of the public WWDC announcement.

title: iCloud announced
description: Steve Jobs announced iCloud at WWDC, replacing MobileMe and providing seamless cloud sync across all Apple devices.

title: Steve Jobs resigns as CEO
description: Steve Jobs resigned as Apple CEO, writing 'I have always said if there ever came a day when I could no longer meet my duties, I would be the first to let you know.' Tim Cook became CEO.

title: Siri and iPhone 4S announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 4S featuring Siri, the first AI voice assistant on a smartphone, at Tim Cook's first keynote.

title: Steve Jobs passes away
description: Steve Jobs passed away at age 56 from complications related to pancreatic cancer. Apple posted a tribute reading 'Apple has lost a visionary and creative genius.'

title: Steve Jobs biography pre-orders surge
description: Walter Isaacson's Steve Jobs biography surged to number one on Amazon following Steve Jobs' death two days earlier.

title: iOS 5 released with iMessage
description: Apple released iOS 5 featuring iMessage, Notification Center, and iCloud integration.

title: iBooks 2 and iTunes U app launched
description: Apple launched iBooks 2 with interactive textbooks and a standalone iTunes U app for educational content.

title: App Store hits 25 billion downloads
description: Apple announced the App Store had surpassed 25 billion downloads, awarding $10,000 in App Store credit to the lucky downloader.

title: iPad with Retina display announced
description: Apple announced the third-generation iPad with a Retina display, A5X chip, and LTE connectivity.

title: New iPad sells 3 million in first weekend
description: Apple announced the third-generation iPad sold 3 million units in its first weekend, a new record.

title: Apple reports best non-holiday quarter ever
description: Apple reported $39.2 billion in revenue for Q2 2012, its best non-holiday quarter in history at the time.

title: Apple becomes most profitable company in history
description: Apple surpassed ExxonMobil to become the most profitable company in US history on an annualized basis.

title: Retina MacBook Pro announced
description: Apple announced the MacBook Pro with Retina display at WWDC, the thinnest and lightest pro Mac notebook ever.

title: iPhone 5 announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 5 with a taller 4-inch screen, Lightning connector, and LTE support.

title: iOS 6 released with Apple Maps
description: Apple released iOS 6, replacing Google Maps with Apple Maps — the launch was widely criticized for poor map data.

title: Tim Cook apologizes for Apple Maps
description: Tim Cook issued a public apology for Apple Maps' poor quality, even recommending competitor apps as alternatives.

title: Apple launches iBooks Author
description: Apple released iBooks Author, a free Mac app for creating interactive digital textbooks for iPad.

title: Apple Maps adds public transit in Japan
description: Apple added public transit directions to Apple Maps in Japan, a key step in rebuilding the app's reputation.

title: Apple releases iWork for iCloud
description: Apple launched iWork for iCloud, allowing Pages, Numbers, and Keynote to run in any web browser.

title: Apple passes 40 billion App Store downloads
description: Apple announced the App Store surpassed 40 billion cumulative downloads, with nearly half occurring in 2012 alone.

title: Roger Rosner becomes VP of Productivity Apps
description: Apple promoted Roger Rosner to VP of Productivity Apps, overseeing iWork and related software.

title: Apple launches iPhone trade-in program
description: Apple launched an iPhone trade-in program in US Apple Stores, offering store credit toward new device purchases.

title: Apple releases iOS 6.1.4
description: Apple released iOS 6.1.4 with an updated speaker phone audio profile for iPhone 5.

title: iOS 7 previewed with flat redesign
description: Apple previewed iOS 7 at WWDC, a complete visual redesign by Jony Ive that replaced skeuomorphism with a flat, translucent aesthetic.

title: iPhone 5c and 5s announced
description: Apple announced the colorful plastic iPhone 5c and the iPhone 5s, the first smartphone with a fingerprint sensor (Touch ID).

title: iPad Air announced
description: Apple announced the iPad Air, 43% thinner than the iPad 3 with a dramatic reduction in size and weight.

title: FaceTime outage affects millions on Christmas Eve
description: A FaceTime service outage affected millions of users trying to video call family on Christmas Eve, lasting several hours.

title: Apple reports best quarter ever
description: Apple reported $57.6 billion in revenue for Q1 2014, selling 51 million iPhones — its best quarter at the time.

title: Apple acquires Beats for $3 billion
description: Apple announced the acquisition of Beats Electronics for $3 billion, its largest acquisition ever at the time.

title: Apple releases iOS 7.1.1
description: Apple released iOS 7.1.1 with Touch ID accuracy improvements and keyboard responsiveness fixes.

title: Apple reports $10.2B quarterly profit
description: Apple reported a $10.2 billion quarterly profit, driven by strong iPhone 5s and 5c sales.

title: Apple releases OS X 10.9.3
description: Apple released OS X Mavericks 10.9.3 with improved 4K display support and Mail updates.

title: Beats acquisition confirmed
description: Apple officially confirmed its $3 billion acquisition of Beats Electronics, its largest purchase in company history.

title: Swift programming language announced
description: Apple announced Swift at WWDC, a new programming language to replace Objective-C for iOS and Mac development.

title: Swift open-sourced announcement hinted
description: Apple developers began pushing for Swift to be open-sourced after its WWDC announcement, a move Apple would later confirm.

title: Apple releases iOS 7.1.2
description: Apple released iOS 7.1.2 fixing a data corruption bug in iCloud backup and Mail attachment issues.

title: Apple acquires Beats officially closes
description: Apple's $3 billion acquisition of Beats Electronics officially closed, with Dr. Dre and Jimmy Iovine joining Apple.

title: iPhone 6 and Apple Watch announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 6, iPhone 6 Plus, and Apple Watch at a special event, the biggest product lineup in years.

title: iPhone 6 goes on sale
description: iPhone 6 and 6 Plus went on sale, with Apple selling 10 million units in the opening weekend, a new record.

title: Apple releases iOS 8.0.2 to fix Touch ID bug
description: Apple released iOS 8.0.2 two days after pulling iOS 8.0.1, which had broken Touch ID and cellular service on iPhone 6.

title: Apple releases iOS 8.0.2
description: Apple released iOS 8.0.2 fixing widespread issues with Touch ID and cellular connectivity introduced in iOS 8.0.1.

title: Apple releases OS X Yosemite 10.10.1
description: Apple released OS X Yosemite 10.10.1 with Wi-Fi reliability improvements and bug fixes.

title: Apple Pay launches
description: Apple Pay launched in the US, allowing iPhone 6 users to make contactless payments using Touch ID.

title: Apple releases iOS 8.1.2
description: Apple released iOS 8.1.2 fixing a bug where ringtones purchased from iTunes were deleted.

title: Apple Watch pre-orders begin
description: Apple opened pre-orders for the Apple Watch, with the most expensive Edition model priced at $17,000.

title: Apple Pay launches in UK
description: Apple Pay expanded internationally, launching in the United Kingdom as its first market outside the United States.

title: Apple Watch gets FDA clearance for heart features
description: Apple Watch health monitoring features received regulatory attention as Apple positioned it as a health and fitness device.

title: Apple Watch shown to press for first time
description: Apple gave selected journalists hands-on time with the Apple Watch ahead of its April launch, receiving widespread positive coverage.

title: Apple Watch goes on sale
description: Apple Watch became available for pre-order and then went on sale starting April 24, 2015, Apple's first new product category since the iPad.

title: Apple Watch gold Edition sells out instantly
description: The $10,000-$17,000 Apple Watch Edition sold out within minutes of pre-orders opening, despite its extreme price.

title: Apple Watch available to try in stores
description: Apple Watch became available for try-on appointments in Apple Stores ahead of its official launch.

title: Apple Watch reviews published
description: Major media outlets published their Apple Watch reviews, with general praise for the device's potential but noting software immaturity.

title: Apple Watch on sale in stores
description: Apple Watch became available in Apple Stores, marking the first time Apple had entered a new product category under Tim Cook.

title: Apple Music details leak ahead of WWDC
description: Details of Apple's upcoming music streaming service leaked ahead of its official announcement at WWDC.

title: Apple acquires Shazam
description: Apple acquired the music recognition app Shazam for a reported $400 million, integrating it into iOS and Siri.

title: Apple Music launches
description: Apple launched Apple Music, a streaming service to compete with Spotify, with a three-month free trial.

title: Taylor Swift convinces Apple to pay artists during trial
description: Taylor Swift published an open letter criticizing Apple Music's free trial policy; Apple reversed course within 24 hours, agreeing to pay artists.

title: Apple Music launches globally
description: Apple Music expanded its launch to over 100 countries, making it available worldwide just over a week after its US launch.

title: Apple Watch OS 2 beta 5 released
description: Apple seeded the fifth beta of watchOS 2 to developers, adding native app support for Apple Watch.

title: Apple releases iOS 8.4.1
description: Apple released iOS 8.4.1 as the final iOS 8 update, fixing bugs ahead of iOS 9's release.

title: Apple releases iOS 8.4.1
description: Apple released a final iOS 8 patch addressing Apple Music and other bug fixes days before iOS 9.

title: iOS 9 released to public
description: Apple released iOS 9 with improved Siri intelligence, iPad multitasking, and News app.

title: iOS 9 released
description: Apple released iOS 9 with a focus on intelligence and proactivity through improved Siri and Spotlight search.

title: iPhone 6s pre-orders break records
description: Apple announced iPhone 6s and 6s Plus broke first-weekend sales records with 13 million units sold.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.1
description: Apple released iOS 9.1 with over 150 new emoji including the middle finger and taco.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.2.1 beta
description: Apple released iOS 9.2.1 beta to developers with bug fixes.

title: App Store sets record on New Year's Day
description: Apple reported that January 1 set a new single-day App Store revenue record as users spent holiday gift cards.

title: Apple reports first iPhone sales decline
description: Apple reported its first ever quarterly iPhone sales decline, ending a remarkable run of consecutive growth.

title: Apple fights FBI over iPhone encryption
description: Apple publicly refused a court order to help the FBI unlock the San Bernardino shooter's iPhone, sparking a national debate on encryption.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.3 with Night Shift
description: Apple released iOS 9.3 beta featuring Night Shift, which automatically warms display colors at night to reduce eye strain.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.3
description: Apple released iOS 9.3 with Night Shift, password-protected Notes, and new 3D Touch shortcuts.

title: iPhone SE announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone SE, a 4-inch iPhone with flagship internals for $399, serving users who preferred smaller phones.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.3.1
description: Apple released iOS 9.3.1 fixing a bug that caused apps to crash when tapping links in Safari and other apps.

title: Apple reports first revenue decline in 13 years
description: Apple reported its first year-over-year revenue decline since 2003, with iPhone sales falling for the first time.

title: Warren Buffett reveals Apple stake
description: Berkshire Hathaway revealed it had purchased approximately $1 billion in Apple stock, a major endorsement from the legendary investor.

title: Apple Pay expands to China
description: Apple Pay launched in China, one of the world's largest mobile payment markets, partnering with China UnionPay.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.3.2
description: Apple released iOS 9.3.2 with bug fixes including a bricking issue affecting some 9.7-inch iPad Pro models.

title: macOS Sierra announced with Siri for Mac
description: Apple announced macOS Sierra at WWDC, bringing Siri to the Mac and renaming OS X to macOS.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.3.3
description: Apple released iOS 9.3.3 with bug fixes and security improvements.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.3.3 to public
description: Apple released iOS 9.3.3 to the public with important security fixes.

title: Apple acquires Turi machine learning startup
description: Apple acquired Turi, a machine learning startup, for approximately $200 million to bolster its AI capabilities.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.3.4
description: Apple released iOS 9.3.4 with an important kernel exploit fix.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.3.5 to patch spyware
description: Apple released an emergency iOS update patching the Trident exploit used by the NSO Group's Pegasus spyware.

title: EU rules Apple owes Ireland $14.5B in taxes
description: The European Commission ruled Apple must pay €13 billion in back taxes to Ireland, calling Apple's tax arrangements illegal state aid.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.3.5 emergency patch
description: Apple pushed an emergency iOS 9.3.5 update to patch the Pegasus spyware exploits discovered by Citizen Lab.

title: iPhone 7 announced without headphone jack
description: Apple announced the iPhone 7, controversially removing the 3.5mm headphone jack, calling it 'courage.'

title: iOS 10 released to public
description: Apple released iOS 10 with a redesigned lock screen, iMessage apps, and an open Siri API for third-party developers.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.0.2
description: Apple released iOS 10.0.2 fixing issues with the headphone adapter and podcast controls.

title: Apple releases macOS Sierra 10.12.1
description: Apple released macOS Sierra 10.12.1 with Unicode 9.0 emoji support and bug fixes.

title: MacBook Pro with Touch Bar announced
description: Apple announced a redesigned MacBook Pro with a Touch Bar OLED strip replacing the function keys.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.1
description: Apple released iOS 10.1 bringing Portrait Mode to iPhone 7 Plus, creating the bokeh camera effect.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.1.1
description: Apple released iOS 10.1.1 fixing a Health data bug that prevented some users from accessing activity data.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.2
description: Apple released iOS 10.2 with a TV app, new emoji, and improvements to Photos.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.2
description: Apple released iOS 10.2 with a new TV app and over 100 new emoji.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.2.1 beta
description: Apple released iOS 10.2.1 beta to developers, addressing unexpected iPhone shutdowns.

title: Apple becomes first company worth $700B
description: Apple's market capitalization surpassed $700 billion, making it by far the most valuable company in history.

title: Apple releases AirPods
description: AirPods finally went on wide sale after delayed availability, quickly becoming one of Apple's most popular accessories.

title: iPad launches in China
description: Apple expanded iPad availability to China, one of its most important and fastest growing markets.

title: New iPad 9.7 inch announced
description: Apple announced a new affordable 9.7-inch iPad for $329, the most affordable full-size iPad ever.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.3.1
description: Apple released iOS 10.3.1 with critical Wi-Fi security fixes for a buffer overflow vulnerability.

title: Apple releases Final Cut Pro X update
description: Apple released a major Final Cut Pro X update with 360-degree video editing and HDR support.

title: Apple acquires Workflow app
description: Apple acquired Workflow, a powerful automation app for iOS, which would later become the Shortcuts app.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.3.2
description: Apple released iOS 10.3.2 with bug fixes and security improvements.

title: Apple releases Final Cut Pro 10.3.2
description: Apple updated Final Cut Pro with performance improvements and bug fixes.

title: HomePod and ARKit announced
description: Apple announced the HomePod smart speaker and ARKit augmented reality platform at WWDC 2017.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.3.3
description: Apple released iOS 10.3.3, the final update for iOS 10, with bug fixes and security improvements.

title: App Store celebrates 9th anniversary
description: The App Store turned 9 years old, with Apple reporting over 180 billion downloads and $70 billion paid to developers.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.3.3
description: Apple released iOS 10.3.3 as a final security update for older devices that could not run iOS 11.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.3.4
description: Apple released iOS 10.3.4 as a GPS date rollover fix for older 32-bit iPhones and iPads.

title: iPhone X announced at Steve Jobs Theater
description: Tim Cook unveiled the iPhone X at the inaugural event in the Steve Jobs Theater, featuring Face ID and an all-screen OLED design.

title: Apple releases iOS 11 to public
description: Apple released iOS 11 with a redesigned App Store, drag and drop on iPad, and ARKit augmented reality framework.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.0.2
description: Apple released iOS 11.0.2 fixing crackling audio during phone calls and issues with email attachments.

title: iMac Pro announced
description: Apple announced the iMac Pro, a pro-focused all-in-one with up to 18-core Xeon processors, starting at $4,999.

title: iPhone X goes on sale
description: The iPhone X went on sale for $999, the most expensive iPhone ever, with long lines at Apple Stores worldwide.

title: iMac Pro goes on sale
description: The iMac Pro went on sale for $4,999, offering the most powerful all-in-one Apple had ever made.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.2
description: Apple released iOS 11.2 with Apple Pay Cash for person-to-person payments and faster wireless charging.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.2.1
description: Apple released iOS 11.2.1 fixing a HomeKit vulnerability that allowed unauthorized smart home device access.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.2.5 beta
description: Apple released iOS 11.2.5 beta introducing HomePod setup and news briefing via Siri.

title: HomePod goes on sale
description: Apple's HomePod smart speaker went on sale for $349, receiving praise for audio quality but criticism for limited Siri features.

title: iCloud data for Chinese users moved to China
description: Apple moved iCloud data for Chinese users to servers inside China, complying with local laws amid privacy criticism.

title: Apple acquires Texture magazine app
description: Apple acquired Texture, a magazine subscription app, which would become the foundation for Apple News+.

title: Apple launches Everyone Can Code curriculum
description: Apple announced Everyone Can Code initiatives at an education event in Chicago, offering free Swift curriculum to schools.

title: Tim Cook testifies before EU Parliament
description: Tim Cook addressed the European Parliament on data privacy, calling for comprehensive US federal privacy legislation.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.3
description: Apple released iOS 11.3 with battery health management tools, following controversy over iPhone performance throttling.

title: Apple reports $61B revenue quarter
description: Apple reported $61.1 billion in quarterly revenue, fueled by iPhone X sales and growing services revenue.

title: Apple reports $52.9B quarter
description: Apple reported $52.9 billion in quarterly revenue and announced a $100 billion share buyback program.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.4
description: Apple released iOS 11.4 bringing AirPlay 2 and stereo HomePod pairing support.

title: Apple reaches $1T market cap milestone in sight
description: Apple's stock climbed above $187, putting the company within striking distance of becoming the first trillion-dollar company.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.4.1 beta
description: Apple released iOS 11.4.1 beta to developers with USB Restricted Mode to improve device security.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.4.1
description: Apple released iOS 11.4.1 introducing USB Restricted Mode, blocking accessory access after an hour of being locked.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.4.1 with security fix
description: Apple released an additional security patch for iOS 11.4.1 addressing vulnerabilities in WebKit.

title: Apple becomes first US company worth $1 trillion
description: Apple's stock briefly crossed $207.05, making it the first US publicly traded company to reach a $1 trillion market capitalization.

title: Apple becomes first trillion-dollar company
description: Apple became the first publicly traded US company to reach a $1 trillion market capitalization.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.4.1 security patch
description: Apple pushed an additional security update addressing WebKit vulnerabilities exploited in the wild.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.4.1 final
description: Apple released the final iOS 11 update before directing users to upgrade to iOS 12.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.4.1 security fix
description: Apple issued a supplemental security update for iOS addressing a critical kernel vulnerability.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.4.1 security update
description: Apple released a final iOS 11 security patch ahead of iOS 12's release.

title: iPhone XS and Apple Watch Series 4 announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone XS, XS Max, XR, and Apple Watch Series 4 with an ECG sensor.

title: iPhone XS goes on sale
description: iPhone XS and XS Max went on sale with Apple's fastest chip ever and improved dual-camera system.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.0.1
description: Apple released iOS 12.0.1 fixing iPhone XS charging issues and restoring the cellular data option in Wi-Fi settings.

title: Apple releases new iPad Pro with Face ID
description: Apple announced a redesigned iPad Pro with Face ID, USB-C, and no home button at a New York event.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.1
description: Apple released iOS 12.1 enabling Group FaceTime for up to 32 people and 70 new emoji.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.1.1
description: Apple released iOS 12.1.1 bringing bug fixes for Face ID and eSIM activation issues.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.1.2
description: Apple released iOS 12.1.2 fixing eSIM activation issues and connectivity bugs in some regions.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.1.3 beta
description: Apple released iOS 12.1.3 beta to developers with HomePod and iPad fixes.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.1.3
description: Apple released iOS 12.1.3 with bug fixes for HomePod, iPad, and FaceTime issues.

title: Apple cuts iPhone XR production orders
description: Reports emerged that Apple had cut iPhone XR production orders, signaling weaker than expected demand.

title: Apple warns of iPhone sales miss
description: Tim Cook issued a rare revenue warning, citing weak iPhone sales in China and causing Apple stock to drop 10%.

title: Group FaceTime bug discovered
description: A major FaceTime bug was discovered that allowed callers to hear audio before the recipient answered, prompting Apple to disable Group FaceTime.

title: Apple launches group subscriptions for Apple Music
description: Apple launched a family plan for Apple Music allowing up to six family members to share a subscription.

title: AirPods 2 announced
description: Apple announced second-generation AirPods with a new H1 chip, hands-free Hey Siri, and an optional wireless charging case.

title: Apple releases AirPods 2
description: Second-generation AirPods went on sale with the new H1 chip enabling faster connections and hands-free Siri.

title: Apple TV+ and Apple Arcade announced
description: Tim Cook announced Apple TV+ streaming service and Apple Arcade gaming subscription at a special Hollywood event.

title: Apple and Qualcomm settle all litigation
description: Apple and Qualcomm settled all ongoing patent disputes worldwide, with Apple agreeing to pay Qualcomm and sign a multi-year chip supply deal.

title: AirPods Pro leaked ahead of announcement
description: Regulatory filings revealed the existence of new AirPods Pro with active noise cancellation ahead of Apple's announcement.

title: Apple releases iTunes 12.9.5
description: Apple released iTunes 12.9.5 with bug fixes, one of the last major iTunes updates before it was discontinued.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.3
description: Apple released iOS 12.3 with a redesigned TV app and Apple TV Channels subscription service.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.3.1
description: Apple released iOS 12.3.1 fixing VoLTE calling issues and a Messages bug affecting some users.

title: Mac Pro redesign teased ahead of WWDC
description: Apple teased a new modular Mac Pro ahead of WWDC, confirming it had abandoned the controversial 2013 trash can design.

title: Mac Pro 2019 goes on sale
description: Apple's new modular Mac Pro went on sale starting at $5,999, with the most maxed-out configuration exceeding $50,000.

title: Apple acquires Intel modem business
description: Apple announced it would acquire the majority of Intel's smartphone modem business for $1 billion, enabling future in-house 5G chips.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.4
description: Apple released iOS 12.4 enabling wireless iPhone-to-iPhone data migration for the first time.

title: Apple reports strong Q3 earnings
description: Apple reported $53.8 billion in Q3 revenue, beating expectations and showing strong Services growth.

title: Apple acquires self-driving car startup Drive.ai
description: Apple acquired Drive.ai, a self-driving car startup, acquiring its engineers and technology for Project Titan.

title: Apple acquires Spectral Edge for camera tech
description: Apple acquired UK startup Spectral Edge, known for image fusion technology that improves low-light photography.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.4.1
description: Apple released iOS 12.4.1 closing a jailbreak vulnerability that had been accidentally re-opened in iOS 12.4.

title: Apple releases macOS 10.14.6 Mojave
description: Apple released macOS Mojave 10.14.6 with bug fixes and security improvements.

title: Apple announces iPhone 11 event
description: Apple sent out invitations for its September 10 event with the tagline 'By innovation only,' confirming the iPhone 11 lineup.

title: iPhone 11 Pro announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 11 Pro with a triple-camera system, the first iPhone with ultra-wide photography.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.1.1
description: Apple released iOS 13.1.1 fixing a bug where third-party keyboard apps could access the internet without permission.

title: Apple TV+ apps launch ahead of service
description: Apple released the standalone Apple TV+ app ahead of the service's November launch, allowing users to prepare their subscriptions.

title: Apple releases 16-inch MacBook Pro
description: Apple released the 16-inch MacBook Pro, addressing widespread complaints about the butterfly keyboard by returning to a scissor mechanism.

title: Apple TV+ launches
description: Apple TV+ launched with original programming including 'The Morning Show' and 'See' for $4.99/month.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.2
description: Apple released iOS 13.2 with Deep Fusion camera processing, HomeKit Secure Video, and 59 new emoji.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.2.3
description: Apple released iOS 13.2.3 fixing issues with background app refresh and mail search.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.3 beta
description: Apple released iOS 13.3 beta to developers, introducing Communication Limits in Screen Time.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.3
description: Apple released iOS 13.3 with Communication Limits in Screen Time and support for NFC, USB, and Lightning security keys.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.3.1 beta
description: Apple released iOS 13.3.1 beta to developers with bug fixes and security improvements.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.3.1
description: Apple released iOS 13.3.1 disabling the Ultra Wideband chip in areas where its use is restricted by law.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.3.1 beta 2
description: Apple continued iterating on iOS 13.3.1 with a second developer beta.

title: Apple closes all stores in China
description: Apple closed all its retail stores and offices in China due to the emerging COVID-19 outbreak.

title: Apple warns COVID-19 will hurt iPhone supply
description: Apple issued a second revenue warning, saying COVID-19 had disrupted iPhone supply chains and reduced demand in China.

title: Apple donates 10 million masks to healthcare workers
description: Tim Cook announced Apple was donating over 10 million masks to healthcare workers during the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Apple closes all stores outside China
description: Apple closed all 458 retail stores outside of Greater China due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Apple and Google announce COVID contact tracing API
description: Apple and Google jointly announced a contact tracing API to help governments build COVID-19 exposure notification apps.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.5 with COVID exposure API
description: Apple released iOS 13.5 enabling the Apple-Google COVID-19 contact tracing API for health authorities worldwide.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.5.1
description: Apple released iOS 13.5.1 patching a serious unc0ver jailbreak vulnerability.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.5.5 beta
description: Apple released a beta with App Clips and other features previewed ahead of iOS 14.

title: Apple Silicon announced
description: Tim Cook announced at WWDC that Apple would transition Macs from Intel to its own ARM-based Apple Silicon chips.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.6
description: Apple released iOS 13.6 adding CarKey support and automatic iOS update controls.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.6 with CarKey
description: Apple released iOS 13.6 adding the CarKey feature, allowing compatible BMWs to be unlocked and started with an iPhone.

title: Tim Cook testifies before Congress on antitrust
description: Tim Cook testified before the House Judiciary Antitrust Subcommittee alongside the CEOs of Amazon, Facebook, and Google.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.6.1
description: Apple released iOS 13.6.1 fixing thermal management issues causing unexpected battery drain.

title: Epic releases hotfix bypassing App Store payments
description: Epic Games released a Fortnite update with direct payment options bypassing Apple's 30% cut, triggering Apple to remove Fortnite from the App Store.

title: Apple removes Fortnite from App Store
description: Apple removed Fortnite from the App Store after Epic's payment workaround, triggering a legal battle that went to trial.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.7
description: Apple released iOS 13.7 with a built-in COVID-19 exposure notification system not requiring a separate app.

title: iPhone 12 and 5G announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 12 lineup with 5G support, a flat-edged design, and MagSafe charging.

title: Apple Watch Series 6 goes on sale
description: Apple Watch Series 6 went on sale with a blood oxygen sensor and always-on altimeter.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.0.1
description: Apple released iOS 14.0.1 fixing issues with default browser and email settings reverting after reboot.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.1
description: Apple released iOS 14.1 with HDR video playback in the Photos app and 4K HDR video in Apple TV.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.2
description: Apple released iOS 14.2 with 100 new emoji, HomePod stereo pair improvements, and new wallpapers.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.2 security update
description: Apple released supplemental iOS 14.2 patches addressing additional vulnerabilities.

title: M1 chip and first Apple Silicon Macs announced
description: Apple announced the M1 chip and first Apple Silicon Macs: the MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, and Mac mini, delivering breakthrough performance.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.2.1
description: Apple released iOS 14.2.1 for iPhone 12 models fixing issues with Messages notifications and MMS.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.2
description: Apple released supplemental security patches alongside iOS 14.2.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.2.1 for older iPhones
description: Apple released iOS 14.2.1 fixes for older iPhone models alongside the iPhone 12-specific patch.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.3
description: Apple released iOS 14.3 enabling ProRAW photography on iPhone 12 Pro and adding the Fitness+ workout service.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.3 with ProRAW
description: iOS 14.3 went live enabling ProRAW photo format on iPhone 12 Pro models for professional photographers.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.3.1 beta
description: Apple seeded iOS 14.3.1 beta to developers shortly after iOS 14.3's release.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.3 security note
description: Apple published the security content for iOS 14.3 detailing all patched vulnerabilities.

title: Apple Car project reported at advanced stage
description: Reports emerged that Apple's secretive electric car project had reached an advanced stage, targeting a 2024 launch.

title: Apple releases AirTag
description: Apple officially announced AirTag at its Spring Loaded event, a precision item tracker using the Find My network.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.4.2
description: Apple released iOS 14.4.2 with an important WebKit security fix addressing an actively exploited vulnerability.

title: AirTag goes on sale
description: Apple's AirTag item tracker went on sale for $29 each or $99 for a four-pack, with millions sold in the first months.

title: M1 iMac and AirTag announced
description: Apple announced a colorful redesigned iMac with M1 chip and the AirTag item tracker at the 'Spring Loaded' event.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.5 with App Tracking Transparency
description: Apple released iOS 14.5 with App Tracking Transparency, requiring apps to ask permission before tracking users across apps.

title: Epic v Apple trial begins
description: The antitrust trial between Epic Games and Apple began in federal court, challenging Apple's App Store commission practices.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.5.1
description: Apple released iOS 14.5.1 fixing an issue where App Tracking Transparency prompts weren't appearing for some users.

title: iOS 15 and FaceTime for Android announced
description: Apple announced iOS 15 at WWDC, including the ability to send FaceTime links to Android and Windows users.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.6
description: Apple released iOS 14.6 enabling Lossless and Spatial Audio in Apple Music, alongside Apple Card Family sharing.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.6 security update
description: Apple released a supplemental security update for iOS 14.6 addressing additional vulnerabilities.

title: App Store celebrates its 13th birthday
description: The App Store marked 13 years since its 2008 launch, having grown to nearly 2 million apps and over 175 billion downloads.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.7
description: Apple released iOS 14.7 with MagSafe Battery Pack support and Apple Card Family account merging.

title: App Store turns 13
description: The App Store celebrated its 13th birthday with Apple reporting $643 billion paid to developers over its lifetime.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.7.1
description: Apple released iOS 14.7.1 fixing an issue that prevented unlocking iPhone with Apple Watch.

title: Apple announces CSAM detection feature
description: Apple announced plans to scan iCloud Photos for child sexual abuse material, sparking widespread privacy debate and later shelving the feature.

title: Apple delays return to office due to Delta variant
description: Apple delayed its planned September return to office indefinitely due to the COVID-19 Delta variant surge.

title: Apple delays CSAM scanning feature
description: Apple announced it was delaying the controversial CSAM photo scanning feature to gather more input from experts and advocacy groups.

title: Epic v Apple ruling released
description: Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers ruled Apple was not a monopolist but ordered it to allow developers to link to external payment methods.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.8 emergency patch
description: Apple released an emergency iOS 14.8 update patching a zero-click iMessage exploit used to deploy Pegasus spyware.

title: iPhone 13 and Apple Watch Series 7 announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 13 lineup with a smaller notch and larger batteries, plus Apple Watch Series 7.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.0.2
description: Apple released iOS 15.0.2 fixing an issue where AirTag accessories could become unresponsive.

title: M1 Pro and M1 Max MacBook Pro announced
description: Apple announced the redesigned 14 and 16-inch MacBook Pro with M1 Pro and M1 Max chips, bringing back MagSafe and HDMI ports.

title: MacBook Pro M1 Pro and M1 Max goes on sale
description: The redesigned 14 and 16-inch MacBook Pro with M1 Pro and M1 Max chips went on sale, receiving universal critical acclaim.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.1
description: Apple released iOS 15.1 enabling SharePlay for FaceTime, ProRes video on iPhone 13 Pro, and COVID vaccine card support in Wallet.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.1.1
description: Apple released iOS 15.1.1 improving call drop rates on iPhone 12 and 13 models.

title: Apple releases macOS 12.1 with SharePlay
description: Apple released macOS Monterey 12.1 enabling SharePlay support for watching content together over FaceTime.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.2
description: Apple released iOS 15.2 with App Privacy Report, Digital Legacy feature, and iCloud Private Relay improvements.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.2.1
description: Apple released iOS 15.2.1 fixing a remote code execution vulnerability in the iCloud storage link sharing feature.

title: Apple hits $3T market cap briefly
description: Apple's stock briefly pushed its market capitalization past $3 trillion for the first time in history.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.3.1
description: Apple released iOS 15.3.1 with an important security fix for a WebKit vulnerability being actively exploited.

title: iPhone SE 3rd generation announced
description: Apple announced the third-generation iPhone SE with 5G support at the 'Peek Performance' event.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.4
description: Apple released iOS 15.4 allowing Face ID to work while wearing a mask, a key pandemic-era accessibility improvement.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.4.1
description: Apple released iOS 15.4.1 fixing rapid battery drain issues reported by users after upgrading to iOS 15.4.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.5
description: Apple released iOS 15.5 with Apple Cash family account improvements and Podcasts storage management.

title: Apple releases Rapid Security Response for the first time
description: Apple debuted its new Rapid Security Response system, allowing critical security patches to be delivered without a full OS update.

title: M2 chip announced
description: Apple announced the M2 chip at WWDC 2022, the first chip built on an enhanced second-generation 5nm process.

title: M2 MacBook Pro and Air announced
description: Apple announced the M2 MacBook Pro and a redesigned M2 MacBook Air at WWDC 2022.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.6 beta
description: Apple released iOS 15.6 beta with Sports tab improvements in the TV app.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.6
description: Apple released iOS 15.6 with live sports streaming in the TV app and various bug fixes.

title: Apple reports record Services revenue
description: Apple reported $19.6 billion in Services revenue for Q3 2022, a record for the category.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.6.1
description: Apple released iOS 15.6.1 fixing two actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in the kernel and WebKit.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.6.1 security update
description: Apple pushed iOS 15.6.1 to address two zero-day vulnerabilities that were being actively exploited.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.6.1 final
description: Apple released the final update before iOS 16, patching remaining security issues in iOS 15.

title: iPhone 14 Pro with Dynamic Island announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 14 Pro featuring Dynamic Island, replacing the notch with an interactive pill-shaped cutout.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.0.1
description: Apple released iOS 16.0.1 fixing a bug with iPhone 14 Pro camera vibration and Exchange account setup issues.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.0.3
description: Apple released iOS 16.0.3 fixing notification delays and low microphone volume during calls on iPhone 14 Pro.

title: Apple becomes defendant in Epic antitrust ruling
description: A US appeals court upheld parts of a ruling requiring Apple to allow developers to link to external payment methods in apps.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.1.1
description: Apple released iOS 16.1.1 with bug fixes for iCloud Keychain and crash detection issues.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.1.2
description: Apple released iOS 16.1.2 with a crash detection improvement for iPhone 14 and an important WebKit security fix.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.2 beta
description: Apple released iOS 16.2 beta introducing Freeform, a collaborative digital whiteboard app.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.2
description: Apple released iOS 16.2 with end-to-end encrypted iCloud backup, the Freeform app, and Apple Music Sing karaoke mode.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.2 security update
description: Apple pushed additional security fixes alongside iOS 16.2.

title: Apple becomes first company worth $3 trillion
description: Apple briefly surpassed a $3 trillion market capitalization, the first company ever to reach that milestone.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.3.1
description: Apple released iOS 16.3.1 fixing critical security vulnerabilities including an actively exploited WebKit zero-day.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.4.1
description: Apple released iOS 16.4.1 with important security fixes for two WebKit vulnerabilities being actively exploited.

title: Apple Vision Pro pre-event press briefings
description: Apple held private briefings for select media ahead of its WWDC keynote, with journalists getting first hands-on time with Vision Pro.

title: Apple Vision Pro announced
description: Tim Cook unveiled the Apple Vision Pro spatial computing headset at WWDC, Apple's first major new product category in nearly a decade.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.5.1
description: Apple released iOS 16.5.1 fixing an actively exploited security vulnerability in WebKit.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.6
description: Apple released iOS 16.6 fixing security vulnerabilities including a zero-click exploit used by the Pegasus spyware.

title: iPhone 15 Pro with titanium announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 15 Pro with a titanium frame, Action button, and USB-C connectivity replacing Lightning.

title: Apple releases iOS 17.0.2
description: Apple released iOS 17.0.2 fixing an issue preventing data transfer from another iPhone during setup.

title: Apple releases iOS 17.0.3
description: Apple released iOS 17.0.3 fixing iPhone 15 Pro overheating issues reported widely after launch.

title: Apple releases iOS 17.0.3
description: Apple released supplemental updates addressing thermal issues on iPhone 15 Pro models.

title: Apple releases iOS 17.1
description: Apple released iOS 17.1 with AirDrop improvements over the internet and Music widget enhancements.

title: Apple releases iOS 17.1.2
description: Apple released iOS 17.1.2 with important security fixes for two WebKit vulnerabilities being actively exploited.

title: Apple releases iOS 17.2
description: Apple released iOS 17.2 with the Journal app, improved Action button options, and Qi2 wireless charging support.

title: Apple releases iOS 17.2.1
description: Apple released iOS 17.2.1 with bug fixes and security improvements shortly after the iOS 17.2 release.

title: Apple Vision Pro goes on sale
description: Apple Vision Pro went on sale for $3,499, with long lines at Apple Stores. Tim Cook was among the first customers.

title: Apple cancels electric car project
description: Apple reportedly cancelled its decade-long electric car project, redirecting hundreds of engineers toward generative AI.

title: iPad Pro with M4 chip announced
description: Apple announced the thinnest Apple product ever — the new iPad Pro with an M4 chip and Ultra Retina XDR OLED display.

title: Apple Intelligence announced
description: Apple announced Apple Intelligence at WWDC 2024, its personal AI system integrated across iPhone, iPad, and Mac.

title: Apple Intelligence beta opens to developers
description: Apple released the first Apple Intelligence beta to developers, allowing testing of AI writing tools, Siri improvements, and Image Playground.

title: iPhone 16 announced with Apple Intelligence
description: Apple announced the iPhone 16 lineup, the first iPhones designed with Apple Intelligence in mind, featuring a new Camera Control button.
